response.justification: Experienced engineer with current Staff-level title and hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators), plus genuine distributed systems pedigree from Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second. Strong TypeScript fits the working-level requirement, and the breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps shows systems thinking. However, Python — a must-have — is conspicuously absent from the skills list and never appears in any role description, which is a material gap for a Staff role where it is the primary language. Worth a screen to verify Python depth and probe stakeholder-management and on-call experience, but not an automatic yes.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-agent/MCP tooling work aligns with AI-native environment expectation
Proven high-throughput distributed systems background (Sociomantic, millions RPS)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js plus polyglot breadth and craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based)
concerns: Python not listed in skills and not referenced in any role — fails a must-have
Limited explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management or technical strategy at scale
No mention of on-call rotation or incident-response ownership
